input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

**XAML â€” use `AppThemeBinding` markup extension:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#444444, Dark=#BBBBBB}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

The framework watches `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` and swaps values automatically â€” no event wiring needed.

---

**C# equivalent â€” use `SetAppTheme` extension method:**

```csharp
// In your page constructor or setup method:
var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Click Me" };
button.SetAppTheme(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"), Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(Button.TextColorProperty, Colors.White, Colors.Black);

// Page background
this.SetAppTheme(BackgroundColorProperty, Colors.White, Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E"));
```

`SetAppTheme(property, lightValue, darkValue)` does the same thing as the XAML markup extension.

---

**Key points:**

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Reactive** | Values update live if the user toggles system theme |
| **Override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of system setting; reset with `AppTheme.Unspecified` |
| **Resources** | For larger apps, define colors in `ResourceDictionary` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` or `{DynamicResource}` instead of inline hex values |
